behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real speech-to-text diarization quality (speaker-label preservation), which a stub transcript would always satisfy. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration is too low for this behavior as written, since the failure can originate in model output quality, not only module wiring. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not strictly required; the behavior does not require validating a multi-step pipeline outcome beyond this single quality boundary. Rejection-gate: Workflow was considered and rejected because the behavior text focuses on preserving speaker labels in STT output, not end-to-end multi-step orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with multiple speakers through the real STT model (diarization enabled), then feed that transcript to summarization and assert attendee attributions in the structured output align with preserved speaker labels. Include at least one sample with overlapping dialogue to expose diarization failure.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, since this is an LLM extraction/coverage quality requirement (who was assigned tasks vs who appears in action items). Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration is too low because deterministic mocks hide omission/attribution quality failures. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not required by the behavior text; it does not require validating a chained multi-stage agent pipeline, only the summary quality criterion. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because no explicit multi-step dependency is stated in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with explicit task assignments to multiple attendees, call the real summarization LLM, and assert each attendee assigned a task appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because this checks semantic extraction and section placement quality of real LLM output. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration is too low; a mocked response can be made perfectly formatted and miss real misclassification/placement failures. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not required; the behavior is a single-output quality check, not a required sequence of multiple agentic stages. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because the behavior text does not require validating chained steps, only section-level correctness in the summary.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts containing explicit decision statements and realistic narrative noise, call the real LLM summarizer, and assert each key decision is present in the structured `decisions` field rather than only free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because this behavior explicitly requires sequential segment processing plus merged coherent summary, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence. Pass 2 downgrade check: Agentic alone is too low because one-call model quality does not validate cross-segment sequencing and merge coherence. Pass 2 upgrade check: no higher level exists. Integration-brake: this is not only module wiring; correctness depends on chained model outputs and merge behavior across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through the production segmentation flow, verify ordered per-segment processing occurs, and validate merged final structured summary preserves continuity (no missing/duplicated cross-segment decisions/action items, consistent attendee references).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, since the behavior explicitly names the full end-to-end pipeline with real recording and multiple AI-dependent stages. Pass 2 downgrade check: System is too low; while real infra matters, the key risk is multi-step agentic pipeline completion across STT and LLM stages. Pass 2 upgrade check: no higher level exists. Integration-brake: real AI outputs and chained steps are explicitly required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run in production-like environment using a real meeting recording: upload, STT call, LLM summarization, and delivery of structured output. Assert completion without human correction/retry and verify output schema is fully populated.
